summary: Iteration 18 backend smoke — Apple + Facebook social login scaffold (credential-gated) covering providers/settings GET+POST, mask-preservation of secrets, and apple/facebook endpoints error paths (503 disabled, 401 invalid). 9/9 pytest tests pass. Cleanup restored apple_enabled=false, facebook_enabled=false (verified via public /providers).

minor.issue: Masking helper uses `s_[:3] + '****' + s_[-3:]` for values with len > 10 — first/last 3 chars of apple_private_key / facebook_app_secret are leaked. Consider showing only fixed '********' or a trailing-4 pattern to avoid surfacing the prefix/suffix of a real secret to admin UI (and in any log/screenshot).

Detection of 'masked' secrets is `'****' in v`. If an admin legitimately wants to save a secret containing four stars (edge case, but possible — e.g. placeholder values during copy-paste from vault), the backend would silently keep the old value instead of updating. Recommend checking for exact-match against the masked pattern returned by GET, or using a dedicated sentinel string.

Apple public-key fetch (https://appleid.apple.com/auth/keys) has no caching. Every real login makes an outbound HTTP call. Not exercised in tests because token parse fails earlier, but worth adding a short in-process TTL cache (e.g. 10 min) when real traffic lands.

facebook_app_secret is included as a URL query parameter in the outbound `graph.facebook.com/oauth/access_token` call. Facebook documents this pattern, but httpx will log the full URL at DEBUG level — if upstream logging is ever bumped to DEBUG, the secret can end up in logs. Consider moving to the `appsecret_proof` pattern.

action_items: Optional: tighten mask helper to stop exposing first/last 3 chars of secrets in GET /auth/social/settings.
Optional: cache Apple JWKS response in-process for ~10 minutes before rolling out real Apple Sign-In.
Optional: switch to appsecret_proof for Facebook Graph API calls to avoid app_secret in query string.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/social_auth.py::get_providers — correctly returns false/false unless BOTH enabled flag AND client_id/app_id are set. Default state verified as {apple:false, facebook:false}.

routes/social_auth.py::admin_save_settings — secret-preservation logic (`if f in SECRETS and (not v or '****' in v): v = existing.get(f, '')`) works for the documented UI flow (re-posting masked value). Verified: re-posting '****' for apple_private_key preserves original so that subsequent /auth/apple call reaches token-parse stage (401 bogus) instead of mis-detecting missing key.

routes/social_auth.py::apple_login — gate order is correct: (1) apple_enabled+client_id → 503, (2) get_unverified_header → 401 on malformed token, (3) JWKS fetch → 401 if kid missing. Bogus token 'bogus' triggers 401 via decoded header parse failure — confirmed.

routes/social_auth.py::facebook_login — on any non-200 from Graph API, raises 401 with truncated (:200) body. `_upsert_social_user` only called after successful token+profile fetch, so cannot be reached with fake code — matches the review_request expectation.

routes/social_auth.py::_upsert_social_user — dedupes by `auth_providers.{provider}.id`, then falls back to email match (links social to existing email account), then creates new customer user. `_id` popped before return — MongoDB ObjectId leak prevented. Not exercised in tests because it requires a real verified Apple sub.

routes/social_auth.py::_issue_jwt — delegates to existing `create_token(user_id, is_admin=False)` so JWT format matches /auth/login output (frontend can treat social login identically). Fallback to a minimal HS256 encode exists but would produce a non-compatible token; worth logging an error rather than silently returning a dissimilar JWT.

server.py — social_auth_router is included via `api_router.include_router(social_auth_router)` so all endpoints resolve at /api/auth/social/* and /api/auth/{apple,facebook}. No conflict with existing /api/auth/login or /api/auth/me.
seed_data_creation: Single `settings` document with id='social_auth' written/overwritten during tests. Autouse fixture resets it to apple_enabled=false, facebook_enabled=false at both setUp and tearDown. Verified post-run via GET /api/auth/social/providers → {apple:false, facebook:false}. No user rows created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iteration18_social_auth.py is self-cleaning (module-scoped autouse fixture disables both providers on teardown). Safe to rerun. Mask-preservation test indirectly verifies original secret survives a masked re-POST by checking that re-enabling apple+using stored private_key allows /auth/apple to reach token-parse (401) rather than 503. Real Apple/Facebook OAuth flow NOT tested — requires real provider creds and is out of scope.
rca of the issue: N/A — all acceptance tests pass. Observations above are optional hardening: mask leaks first/last 3 chars, JWKS not cached, fb app_secret goes via query string.
